title: v0.14.0 release notes, reviewed line by line
intro: The draft below is the full release-notes text — select any line range and leave a note, and I fold every note into the next pass. Under it, the changelog entries: keep or cut each one. Submit when every entry has a verdict.

submit.label: Send review
submit.note: Submitting sends your line notes and entry verdicts; I redraft and open another round.

blocks.title: Release notes, first pass
blocks.text:
## v0.14.0

cc-present grows two review primitives.

The draft block renders a document as numbered lines and streams your
line-anchored notes back to the agent, surviving redrafts by re-anchoring
on content instead of position.

The triage block turns a list into per-item accept/reject calls, with
bulk controls and per-item notes.

Both land across the web board and the native iOS client in one release.

blocks.prompt: Sign off on this pass of the notes?
Keep or cut each changelog entry?

items.label: draft: line-annotatable documents
items.md: A document block the human marks up with line-anchored notes.

items.label: triage: per-item accept/reject
items.md: A list block with independent verdicts, bulk controls, and per-item notes.

items.label: Line-anchor wire contract
items.md: The ccx anchor scheme, ported to Go, TypeScript, and Swift with one shared conformance corpus.

items.label: Internal: generalized round-snapshot filter
items.hint: maybe too internal for the changelog
items.md: One generic filter with per-value clone hooks replaced the per-map helpers.
